file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the Notifications page of the application. Contains locators for notification-related UI elements including the notifications table, notification box, notification icon, and table rows. Provides a property method to access the notifications table element for test interactions.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Notifications page. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns. Defines locators for the notifications table, table rows, notifications box, notifications icon, and notifications table using data-testid attributes. Provides a property method to access the first matching notifications table element for reading notification data in tests.

locators.ui_element_description: Main notifications table element that displays the list of notifications in a tabular format. Uses jarvis-notifications-table data-testid attribute.
Individual table row elements within the notifications table. Constructed by appending 'tr' selector to the table selector to target all row elements.
Notifications center box container element that holds the notifications interface. Uses portal-notifications-center data-testid attribute.
Notifications icon button that users click to open or access the notifications panel. Uses portal-notifications-button data-testid attribute.
Composite selector that matches either the jarvis notifications table or the portal notifications center. Provides flexibility to locate notifications in different UI contexts.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the NotificationsPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where notifications are displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the NotificationsPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines five notification-related locators as attributes on the self.elements object. Sets self.elements.table to the jarvis-notifications-table data-testid selector. Constructs self.elements.table_row by concatenating the table selector with ' tr' to target table rows. Sets self.elements.notifications_box to the portal-notifications-center data-testid selector. Sets self.elements.notifications_icon to the portal-notifications-button data-testid selector. Finally sets self.elements.notifications_table to a composite selector that matches either jarvis-notifications-table or portal-notifications-center. All locators are stored on the elements object for access by other methods.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the NotificationsPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the notifications table element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.table selector string to create a locator for the jarvis-notifications-table element. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple tables exist on the page. This property allows test code to access the notifications table using notifications_page.table syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used for further interactions like clicking, getting text, or waiting for visibility.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a minimal page object with only locator definitions and one property getter. It follows the Page Object Model pattern by extending BasePageObject. All locators use data-testid attributes which are reliable for test automation. The table property provides convenient access to the notifications table element. The page object uses the self.elements pattern to store locators, which is consistent with the BasePageObject architecture. No action methods are defined - this suggests that notification interactions may be handled by other helper methods or that this page object is primarily used for verification rather than actions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the OSS (Online Support System) Emulator interface used for testing printer setup, configuration, and enrollment flows. Contains extensive locators for emulator controls including dev tab elements, device configuration fields, app settings, offer acceptance buttons, post-OOBE elements, and printer replacement options. Used to simulate printer onboarding and configuration scenarios in test environments.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and controls for the OSS Emulator testing interface. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 50+ locators organized by functional sections: Dev Tab for login and authentication, Quick Options for preset configurations, Device Tab for printer hardware settings, App Tab for application type selection, Page Elements for flow control, Offers Elements for HP Plus and subscription offers, and Post-OOBE Elements for post-setup configuration. Used extensively in test automation to simulate printer setup flows without requiring physical printer hardware.

locators.ui_element_description: Dev tab navigation link in the OSS emulator interface. Clicking this tab displays developer and authentication options.
OSS HPID login button or field for initiating HP ID authentication in the emulator.
Sign in button to submit authentication credentials in the OSS emulator login flow.
Username input field for entering the user's email or username during OSS emulator authentication.
Submit button for the username form step in the OSS emulator authentication process.
Password input field for entering the user's password during OSS emulator authentication.
Novelli button in the OSS emulator, likely related to a specific test configuration or environment setting.
Navigation link to access App/Post-OOBE/Agreements/Features section in the OSS emulator for configuring post-setup options.
Yeti 3m quick option button for selecting a preset 3-month configuration in the OSS emulator.
Serial number input field in the Device Tab for entering the printer's serial number.
SKU input field in the Device Tab for entering the printer's stock keeping unit identifier.
UUID input field in the Device Tab for entering the printer's universally unique identifier.
Cloud ID input field in the Device Tab for entering the printer's cloud identifier using Angular ng-model binding.
CDM printer fingerprint input field in the Device Tab for entering the printer's unique fingerprint identifier.
Registration state dropdown or field in the Device Tab for setting the printer's registration status.
Usage tracking consent checkbox or toggle in the Device Tab for enabling/disabling usage data collection.
Language configuration dropdown in the Device Tab for setting the printer's language preference.
Country configuration dropdown in the Device Tab for setting the printer's country/region.
Load main tray button or checkbox in the Device Tab for simulating paper loading in the printer's main tray.
Insert ink button or checkbox in the Device Tab for simulating ink cartridge installation.
Calibration button or checkbox in the Device Tab for simulating printer calibration process.
App tab navigation element for accessing application-related configuration options in the OSS emulator.
App type dropdown or selector in the App Tab for choosing the application type for the test scenario.
Text label 'App Type' used to identify or locate the app type field in the interface.
Start flow button that initiates the printer setup or enrollment flow in the OSS emulator.
Accept all button for consenting to all offers, agreements, or privacy settings in the setup flow.

Activate HP Plus button for accepting and activating the HP Plus subscription offer.
Do not activate HP Plus button that shows the decline confirmation dialog. Uses Angular ng-click directive.
Decline HP Plus button for rejecting the HP Plus subscription offer.
Dynamic security notice continue button for HP Plus that acknowledges the security notice and continues the flow.
Generic next button for advancing to the next step in multi-step flows.
Learn more button that provides additional information about offers or features.
Value proposition title element that displays the heading for offer value propositions.
Instant Ink reminder element that displays reminder information about the Instant Ink service.
Learn more button specifically for value proposition sections. Same selector as learn_more_button.
Ink subscription checkbox in post-OOBE settings. Targets the span element adjacent to the Angular-bound input for clicking.

Replace printer radio button icon for selecting the printer replacement option.
Continue button with analytics tracking ID for advancing through the flow.
Continue selection button styled as primary button with Angular binding and enabled state.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OssEmulatorPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the OSS emulator interface is loaded

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OssEmulatorPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines 52 locators organized into logical sections. First defines Dev Tab locators including dev_tab, oss_hpid_login, sign_in_button, username_input, user_name_form_submit, password_input, novelli_button, and app_post_oobe_agreements_features for authentication and navigation. Then defines Quick Options locators like yeti_3m for preset configurations. Next defines extensive Device Tab locators including serial_number, sku, uuid, cloud_id, fingerprint, registration_state, usage_tracking_consent, language_config, country_config, load_main_tray, insert_ink, and calibration for device configuration. Defines App Tab locators including app_tab, app_type, and app_type_label for application settings. Defines Page Elements like start_flow_button for flow control. Defines extensive Offers Elements including accept_all_button, Hp_Plus_Continue_button, Activate_HP_Plus, do_not_activate_hp_plus, decline_hp_plus, ds_continue_button, next_button, learn_more_button, value_prop_title, instant_ink_reminder, and value_prop_learn_more for handling subscription offers. Defines Post-OOBE Elements including subscription_checkbox and oobe_checkbox for post-setup configuration. Finally defines printer replacement elements including replace_printer_radio_button, continue_button, and continue_selection. All locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive page object for the OSS Emulator testing interface with 52 locators but no action methods defined. The page object is purely a locator repository, suggesting that interactions with the emulator are handled by helper methods or test scripts directly. The locators are well-organized with comments grouping them by functional area (Dev Tab, Device Tab, App Tab, Offers, Post-OOBE). The mix of selector strategies (ID, XPath, CSS, data-testid, Angular directives) reflects the diverse nature of the emulator interface. Many locators use Angular-specific attributes (ng-click, ng-model) indicating the emulator is built with AngularJS. The extensive offer-related locators (HP Plus activation, decline, dynamic security) suggest this emulator is heavily used for testing subscription enrollment flows. The presence of device configuration locators (serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, fingerprint) indicates this emulator simulates printer hardware characteristics for testing.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the main Overview/Dashboard page of the Instant Ink application. This is the primary landing page after login where users see their printer status, ink levels, plan details, billing information, and subscription management options. Contains 150+ locators covering all major sections including status cards, printer information, ink cartridge status, plan details, payment and shipping information, special offers, privacy settings, tour elements, footer links, and paper subscription features. Provides methods for accessing key page elements and properties.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the main Overview/Dashboard page of the Instant Ink application. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 150+ locators organized into functional sections: enrollment and printer replacement, status card elements, printer information and selector, printer details modal, ink cartridge status, plan details and billing, promo codes and special offers, first access and onboarding elements, privacy settings, ink cartridge items, pause/resume plan functionality, tour tooltips, alert messages, footer links, unsubscribed status, support card, PaaS banner, emergency banners, paper subscription elements, and various action buttons. Provides getter methods and properties for accessing frequently used elements like page title, change plan link, printer name, and RAF code. This is the most complex page object in the framework due to the comprehensive nature of the overview page.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the overview/dashboard is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the OverviewPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines 157 locators organized into logical functional sections. Defines enrollment and printer replacement locators including enroll_or_replace_button, enroll_printer_button, replace_printer_button, finished_replacing_printer_modal, complete_enrollment_button, connect_later_button, printer_replacement_page, and enroll_modal_close. Defines extensive status card locators including status_card, status_card_body, status_card_title, status_card_cloud_kick, status_card_printer_name, printer_details_link, view_print_history_link, monthly_summary, and multiple progress bars for plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, credited pages, additional pages, and complimentary pages. Defines printer information locators including printer_selector, printer_selector_first_printer, printer_selector_printers, printer_offline_message, connectivity_guide_link, printers_grouped_by_status, and printer_selector_option. Defines printer details modal locators. Defines ink cartridge status card locators. Defines comprehensive plan details card locators including subscription_id, plan_information, free_months, paper_free_months, billing_section_plan_details, change_billing_link, shipping_section_plan_details, change_shipping_link, change_plan_link, special_offers_balance, redeem_code_link, cancel_instant_ink, transfer_subscription_link, pause_plan_link, pause_plan_info, add_payment_method_link, no_active_plans, keep_enrollment_button, paypal_info, message_add_billing_info, alert_message, critical_message, and savings calculator elements. Defines promo code modal locators. Defines special offers card locators including raf_card, special_offer_card, and raf_code. Defines first access elements for onboarding. Defines privacy consent elements. Defines ink cartridge item locators for individual color cartridges. Defines pause plan modal and related elements. Defines tour tooltip elements. Defines alert message locators. Defines footer link locators. Defines unsubscribed status card elements. Defines support card elements. Defines PaaS banner elements. Defines emergency banner elements with color variations. Defines extensive paper subscription elements including status, summary, billing, shipment, and cancellation locators. Defines various action buttons and modals. All 157 locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object and test scripts.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to appear on the overview page and returns the element. Calls self.page.wait_for_selector() with the self.elements.page_title selector (which is '#rebranded-home-page') and the provided timeout value. This method is typically used to verify that the overview page has fully loaded before proceeding with other interactions. Returns the Playwright ElementHandle for the page title element once it becomes available within the timeout period. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will raise a TimeoutError.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the change plan link element from the overview page. Calls self.page.get_by_test_id() with the self.elements.change_plan_link selector value (which is '[data-testid='change-plan-link'] > span') to locate the element using Playwright's get_by_test_id method. Then calls .first on the result to return only the first matching element if multiple exist. Returns a Playwright Locator object for the change plan link that can be used for further interactions like clicking or getting text. This method provides convenient access to the change plan link without requiring test code to know the exact selector.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the printer name element from the status card on the overview page. Calls self.page.get_by_test_id() with the self.elements.status_card_printer_name selector value (which is 'h2[data-testid='printer-name']') to locate the H2 heading element displaying the printer name. Then calls .first on the result to return only the first matching element. Returns a Playwright Locator object for the printer name element that can be used to get the text content or verify visibility. This method is useful for verifying which printer is currently selected or displayed in the status card.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the OverviewPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the refer-a-friend (RAF) code element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.raf_code selector (which is '[class^="referAFriendPromoContentNewVisId__share-url"]') to create a locator for the RAF share URL element. Then calls .last on the locator to return the last matching element if multiple exist (since RAF codes may appear in multiple places). This property allows test code to access the RAF code using overview_page.raf_code syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used to get the text content of the referral code or URL for sharing.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most comprehensive page object in the batch with 157 locators covering every section of the overview/dashboard page. The page object follows the Page Object Model pattern by extending BasePageObject and using the self.elements pattern for locator storage. The locators are well-organized with inline comments grouping them by functional area. The page uses primarily data-testid selectors which are the most reliable for test automation, with some CSS class selectors for dynamically styled elements. The page object includes 4 getter methods and 1 property for convenient access to frequently used elements. The extensive locator coverage reflects the complexity of the overview page which serves as the main hub for all Instant Ink subscription management activities including printer status, ink levels, plan details, billing, shipping, offers, privacy settings, paper subscription, and more. The presence of multiple modal-related locators (promo code, pause plan, printer details, enrollment, paper offers) indicates this page has many interactive flows. The paper subscription locators suggest this is a newer feature with its own dedicated UI section. The tour and first-access elements indicate comprehensive onboarding for new users. This page object would be heavily used across all test scenarios as it is the primary landing page after login.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the version 3 plan selector interface for choosing Instant Ink subscription plans. Contains locators for the plan selector dropdown, plan options, ink-only and ink-and-paper selection buttons, content area, continue button, and header elements. Provides a select_plan method to choose a specific plan by page value using XPath locator strategy.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the version 3 plan selector page where users choose their Instant Ink subscription plan. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines locators for the plan selector combobox, listbox, plan options, ink-and-paper button, ink-only button, content area, continue button, and header elements including virtual agent link, support phone number, country selector, and account button. Also defines locators for pay-as-you-print and monthly plan cards. Provides a select_plan method that takes a plan value (like '10', '50', '100') and clicks the corresponding plan option using an XPath selector with logging.

locators.ui_element_description: Version 3 plan selector combobox dropdown for selecting subscription plans.
Listbox container that appears when the plan selector dropdown is opened, containing all plan options.
Individual plan option elements within the listbox. Each option represents a different page count plan.
Button to select a plan that includes both ink and paper subscription.
Button to select a plan that includes only ink subscription without paper.
Main content area of the version 3 plan selector page displaying plan information and options.
Continue button span element to proceed after selecting a plan.
Header link to access the virtual agent chatbot for help.
Header link displaying the support phone number for customer service.
Header button to open the country/region selector dropdown.
Header button to access account settings and options.
Card displaying the pay-as-you-print plan type option.
Card displaying the monthly ink subscription plan type option.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the plan selector v3 interface is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PlanSelectorV3Page object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then defines 13 locators for the plan selector interface. Sets self.elements.plans_selector_v3 to the plan selector combobox data-testid. Sets self.elements.plans_listbox_v3 to the role='listbox' selector for the dropdown list. Sets self.elements.plan_option to role='option' for individual plan options. Sets self.elements.ink_paper_button and self.elements.ink_only_button for the two plan type selection buttons. Sets self.elements.content_area_v3 for the main content area. Sets self.elements.continue_button for the proceed button. Defines four header element locators: virtual_agent_link, support_phone_number_link, country_selector_button, and account_button. Finally defines two plan card locators: pay_as_you_print_plan_card and monthly_plan_card. All locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PlanSelectorV3Page object

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: The plan page count value to select, such as '10', '50', '100', '300', '700'. This is the number of pages included in the plan.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific subscription plan from the plan selector dropdown by clicking the option that contains the provided plan_value. Constructs an XPath selector using f-string formatting: '//*[@role="option" and contains(normalize-space(.),"{plan_value} ")]' which finds an element with role='option' whose normalized text content contains the plan value followed by a space. Logs an info message using framework_logger.info() stating which plan is being selected with the constructed selector. Then calls self.page.locator(option_selector).click() to locate the plan option and click it. Logs another info message confirming the plan selection was clicked successfully. This method handles the plan selection interaction so test code can simply call select_plan('100') to choose the 100-page plan without needing to know the exact selector logic.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object represents the version 3 plan selector interface with 13 locators and 1 action method. The select_plan method is the key reusable component that encapsulates the logic for selecting a plan by page count value. The method uses XPath with normalize-space and contains functions to handle text matching, which is more robust than exact text matching. The method includes logging statements using framework_logger which helps with debugging and test execution tracking. The commented-out line in select_plan shows an alternative selector approach that was not used, likely because it had issues with certain plan values like '10'. The page object includes header elements (virtual agent, support phone, country selector, account button) which are common across multiple pages, suggesting these may be part of a shared header component. The presence of both pay_as_you_print_plan_card and monthly_plan_card locators indicates the page supports multiple plan types. The ink_paper_button and ink_only_button locators suggest users can choose between ink-only and ink-plus-paper subscriptions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object representing the Print History page where users can view their printing activity, billing history, invoices, and usage statistics. Contains locators for page elements, progress bars showing page usage, plan details card, FAQ section with questions and answers, billing cycle selector, print history table, and various links for downloading invoices and managing plans. Provides property methods for accessing frequently used elements.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and interactions for the Print History page where users view their printing activity and billing information. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. Defines 40+ locators organized into sections: page elements (title, card, links), progress bars (plan pages, trial pages, rollover pages, additional pages), cards (plan details, FAQ), FAQ questions and answers (4 questions with expandable answers), FAQ links (terms of service, overview, update plan), table elements (history table, date column, description column, invoice links, billing cycle selector), and various text elements displaying usage statistics and billing amounts. Provides two property methods (faq_update_plan_link and plan_price_text) for convenient access to frequently used elements with .first filtering.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the print history is displayed

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the PrintHistoryPage object by calling the parent BasePageObject constructor with super().__init__(page) to set up the page instance. Then systematically defines 44 locators organized into logical sections. Defines page elements including page_title, print_history_card, print_history_card_title, how_is_calculated_link, total_printed_pages, free_months, print_history_section, plan_details_card, and account_suspended_banner. Defines progress bar locators for plan_pages_bar, trial_pages_bar, rollover_pages_bar, and additional_pages_message. Defines card locators including a duplicate plan_details_card and faq_card. Defines four FAQ question locators (faq_question_1 through faq_question_4) using aria-controls attributes and four corresponding FAQ answer locators (faq_answer_1 through faq_answer_4) using ID selectors. Defines FAQ link locators including print_faq, faq_terms_of_service_link, faq_overview_link, update_plan_link, and print_history_submenu. Defines extensive table element locators including print_history_table_button, print_history_table, table_date, table_description, table_invoice, table_selector, table_selector_list, billing_cycle_option, billing_cycle_options, select_1_option, select_2_option, select_3_option, and billing_cycle_period_title. Defines text element locators for displaying usage and billing information including total_pages_printed, plan_pages_text, rollover_pages_text, additional_pages_text, plan_price_text, overage_description_text, previous_billing_text, current_billing_text, and current_total_text. Finally defines download_all_invoices_button, payment_problem_banner, print_history_table_description, and paper_summary. All 44 locators are stored on the self.elements object for access throughout the page object.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the update plan link within the FAQ section. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.update_plan_link selector (which is '[data-testid='print-history-faq-2-change-plan-link']') to create a locator for the change plan link in the FAQ. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple exist. This property allows test code to access the FAQ update plan link using print_history_page.faq_update_plan_link syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used for clicking or verifying the link.

parameters.name: self
parameters.description: Instance reference to the PrintHistoryPage object

functions.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the plan price description text element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.plan_price_text selector (which is '[data-testid="plan-description"]') to create a locator for the plan description element that displays plan pricing information. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element if multiple exist. This property allows test code to access the plan price text using print_history_page.plan_price_text syntax without calling it as a method. Returns a Playwright Locator object that can be used to get the text content or verify the plan pricing information displayed.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object represents the Print History page with 44 locators and 2 property methods. The page is well-organized with locators grouped by functional area. The FAQ section has a structured approach with 4 questions and 4 answers, each with dedicated locators using aria-controls for questions and ID selectors for answers. The table section has comprehensive locators for all table components including columns, selectors, and billing cycle options. The presence of multiple progress bar locators (plan, trial, rollover, additional) indicates detailed tracking of page usage across different categories. The billing-related text locators (plan_price_text, overage_description_text, previous_billing_text, current_billing_text, current_total_text) suggest this page provides detailed billing breakdowns. The two property methods (faq_update_plan_link and plan_price_text) provide convenient access to frequently used elements with .first filtering to handle potential multiple matches. The paper_summary locator indicates integration with paper subscription features. The payment_problem_banner and account_suspended_banner locators suggest the page handles error states. The download_all_invoices_button indicates bulk invoice download functionality. This page object would be used in tests that verify billing history, invoice generation, usage tracking, and FAQ content.
